name: Oakwood Feed Store Chili
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds Lean beef chuck roast, cubed
4 tablespoons Prepared garlic in oil (olive oil)
4 ounces Canned chopped chilies, El Paso brand
6 Fresh Jalapeno chilies, seeded and chopped
5 tablespoons Freshly ground DRY Mexican chili powder (ancho if unavailable)
2 tablespoons Freshly ground DRY Ancho chili powder
2 tablespoons Good quality chili pepper (such as guajillo)
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry bacon in a heavy pot until crisp and the fat is rendered. Remove and reserve the bacon.
2. Pour off most of the bacon fat into a heavy cast iron skillet, leaving a small amount in the pot.
3. Brown the meat (beef chuck roast and pork roast) and garlic in the skillet.
4. Saute onions in the fat remaining in the pot until soft.
5. Add the meat, bacon, ground chilies, dried spices, sauces, green chilies, Jalapenos, stock, and tomatoes to the pot.
6. Simmer for two hours.
7. Allow the chili to sit in the refrigerator for 24 hours to let the flavors intensify.
8. Garnish with fresh chopped Jalapenos, chopped raw onions, shredded Monterey or Sharp Cheddar cheese, and saltines.
